page: Microsoft Azure D8plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpldsv6 server family. It is powered by a Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor featuring 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The architecture does not support hyperthreading, allocating exactly one thread per core. Memory capacity is configured at 16.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 472 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark evaluations position this server primarily in the average performance tier for most workloads. The stress-ng single-core CPU score is 2073.29, and the multi-core score is 16574.2. Memory bandwidth tests show a cached read-only small block speed of 910.4 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds reach 169.64 GB/sec and 72.69 GB/sec, respectively. Notably, the server demonstrates strong single-threaded gzip compression performance at 33.05 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25% tier. However, cryptographic performance is a relative bottleneck, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring 1492.59, which falls into the bottom 25% tier.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D8plds_v6 offers a compute-focused hardware profile with a lower memory footprint, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require dedicated physical cores without massive memory overhead. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight database operations. It is also capable of handling small-scale language model inference, processing 434.6 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 135M model.
description: Microsoft Azure D8plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dpldsv6 family, featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. With a compute-focused 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it provides 16.0 GB of memory alongside 472 GB of local storage and Premium Storage support. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for most operations, highlighted by a strong single-threaded gzip compression speed of 33.05 GB/sec, though cryptographic performance is weaker, scoring 1492.59 on PassMark encryption. The server is optimized for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources with low memory overhead, such as software compilation, static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage, optimized for compute-focused workloads and static web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8plds_v6 is an ARM-based VM with 8 dedicated cores, 16 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage, built for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores and a compute-optimized memory ratio for efficient web serving and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 cores running at 3.4 GHz
16.0 GB of RAM providing a compute-optimized 1:2 resource ratio
472 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance of 33.05 GB/sec
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and Redis databases
